TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_abinung, title = abinung, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/abinung} }

S.FORM: == abinung == tadatakalaw ku langaw nina abinung. tabaki ku heci nu abinung. u pudac nu abinung sa u landaway. a'su mukan nu labu'ay. sikabi, siheci. u sangelacay ku ilabu'ay. katuud ku pacakay abinung i Pingtung. hali caledesay a demaid. anu mahetik i bayu', taneng munabaw i bayu' tayza i baat a subal katakit. i Taykoku muculcul tu ungay mukayakay tu abinung tapabaw miala th heci. u cudadan a ngangan sa u Cocos nucifera. u sulit nu zuma a kamu u sulit nu Hulam . u sulit nu Amilika The coconut tree (Cocos nucifera) is a member of the family Arecaceae and the only species of the genus Cocos. The term coconut can refer to the whole coconut palm or the seed, or the fruit, which, botanically, is a drupe, not a nut. The spelling cocoanut is an archaic form of the word. The term is derived from the 16th-century Portuguese and Spanish word coco meaning "head" or "skull", from the three indentations on the coconut shell that resemble facial features.
S.PHON: == abinuŇč == tadatakar~…ĺaw ku r~…ĺaŇčaw nina abinuŇč. tabaki ku ńß…ô ¶i nu abinuŇč. u puda ¶ nu abinuŇč sa u r~…ĺandawaj. a Ē~ °su mukan nu r~…ĺabu Ē~ °aj. sikabi, sińß…ô ¶i. u saŇč…ôr~…ĺa ¶aj ku ir~…ĺabu Ē~ °aj. katuud ku pa ¶akaj abinuŇč i piŇčtuŇč. ńßar~…ĺi ¶ar~…ĺ…ôd…ôsaj a d…ômaid. anu mańß…ôtik i baju Ē~ °, tan…ôŇč munabaw i baju Ē~ ° tajza i baat a subar~…ĺ katakit. i tajkoku mu ¶ur~…ĺ ¶ur~…ĺ tu uŇčaj mukajakaj tu abinuŇč tapabaw miar~…ĺa tńß ńß…ô ¶i. u ¶udadan a ŇčaŇčan sa u ¶o ¶os nu ¶if…ôra. u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam . u sur~…ĺit nu amir~…ĺika tńß…ô ¶o ¶onut tr…ô…ô ( ¶o ¶os nu ¶if…ôra) is a m…ômb…ôr of tńß…ô famir~…ĺj ar…ô ¶a ¶…ôa…ô and tńß…ô onr~…ĺj sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tńß…ô g…ônus ¶o ¶os. tńß…ô t…ôrm ¶o ¶onut ¶an r…ôf…ôr to tńß…ô wńßor~…ĺ…ô ¶o ¶onut par~…ĺm or tńß…ô s…ô…ôd, or tńß…ô fruit, wńßi ¶ńß, botani ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj, is a drup…ô, not a nut. tńß…ô sp…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺiŇč ¶o ¶oanut is an ar ¶ńßai ¶ form of tńß…ô word. tńß…ô t…ôrm is d…ôri*…ôd from tńß…ô **tńß- ¶…ônturj portugu…ôs…ô and spanisńß word ¶o ¶o m…ôaniŇč "ńß…ôad" or "skur~…ĺr~…ĺ", from tńß…ô tńßr…ô…ô ind…ôntations on tńß…ô ¶o ¶onut sńß…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ tńßat r…ôs…ômbr~…ĺ…ô fa ¶iar~…ĺ f…ôatur…ôs.
S.FORM: == abinung == tadatakalaw ku langaw nina abinung. tabaki ku heci nu abinung. u pudac nu abinung sa u landaway. a'su mukan nu labu'ay. sikabi, siheci. u sangelacay ku ilabu'ay. katuud ku pacakay abinung i Pingtung. hali caledesay a demaid. anu mahetik i bayu', taneng munabaw i bayu' tayza i baat a subal katakit. i Taykoku muculcul tu ungay mukayakay tu abinung tapabaw miala th heci. u cudadan a ngangan sa u Cocos nucifera. u sulit nu zuma a kamu u sulit nu Hulam . u sulit nu Amilika The coconut tree (Cocos nucifera) is a member of the family Arecaceae and the only species of the genus Cocos. The term coconut can refer to the whole coconut palm or the seed, or the fruit, which, botanically, is a drupe, not a nut. The spelling cocoanut is an archaic form of the word. The term is derived from the 16th-century Portuguese and Spanish word coco meaning "head" or "skull", from the three indentations on the coconut shell that resemble facial features.
S.PHON: == abinuŇč == tadatakar~…ĺaw ku r~…ĺaŇčaw nina abinuŇč. tabaki ku ńß…ô ¶i nu abinuŇč. u puda ¶ nu abinuŇč sa u r~…ĺandawaj. a Ē~ °su mukan nu r~…ĺabu Ē~ °aj. sikabi, sińß…ô ¶i. u saŇč…ôr~…ĺa ¶aj ku ir~…ĺabu Ē~ °aj. katuud ku pa ¶akaj abinuŇč i piŇčtuŇč. ńßar~…ĺi ¶ar~…ĺ…ôd…ôsaj a d…ômaid. anu mańß…ôtik i baju Ē~ °, tan…ôŇč munabaw i baju Ē~ ° tajza i baat a subar~…ĺ katakit. i tajkoku mu ¶ur~…ĺ ¶ur~…ĺ tu uŇčaj mukajakaj tu abinuŇč tapabaw miar~…ĺa tńß ńß…ô ¶i. u ¶udadan a ŇčaŇčan sa u ¶o ¶os nu ¶if…ôra. u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam . u sur~…ĺit nu amir~…ĺika tńß…ô ¶o ¶onut tr…ô…ô ( ¶o ¶os nu ¶if…ôra) is a m…ômb…ôr of tńß…ô famir~…ĺj ar…ô ¶a ¶…ôa…ô and tńß…ô onr~…ĺj sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tńß…ô g…ônus ¶o ¶os. tńß…ô t…ôrm ¶o ¶onut ¶an r…ôf…ôr to tńß…ô wńßor~…ĺ…ô ¶o ¶onut par~…ĺm or tńß…ô s…ô…ôd, or tńß…ô fruit, wńßi ¶ńß, botani ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj, is a drup…ô, not a nut. tńß…ô sp…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺiŇč ¶o ¶oanut is an ar ¶ńßai ¶ form of tńß…ô word. tńß…ô t…ôrm is d…ôri*…ôd from tńß…ô **tńß- ¶…ônturj portugu…ôs…ô and spanisńß word ¶o ¶o m…ôaniŇč "ńß…ôad" or "skur~…ĺr~…ĺ", from tńß…ô tńßr…ô…ô ind…ôntations on tńß…ô ¶o ¶onut sńß…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ tńßat r…ôs…ômbr~…ĺ…ô fa ¶iar~…ĺ f…ôatur…ôs.
